title: hieracium murorum subsp. oxyodontopsis
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. oxyodontopsis, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and reducing inflammation, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Hieracium murorum subsp. oxyodontopsis has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Wall Hawkweed can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which is said to aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
